body.title: Procedural habit: annotate new and existing engrams with purpose fields
body.result: purpose_annotator.py + coa.py wiring + PROC_PURPOSE_ANNOTATOR seeded; annotates 2 engrams per NE run
body.description:
Igor needs a self-annotation habit that fires at engram creation time and writes purpose, purpose_category, and purpose_embedding by reasoning about the engram's content. The same habit provides a retroactive sweep mode that processes existing unannotated engrams in batches (paced to avoid overwhelming LLM budget).

The habit is itself an engram — Igor's self-knowledge about how to annotate his own knowledge structures lives inside his knowledge system.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/habits/ (new habit), wild_igor/igor/memory/ (creation hook wiring), wild_igor/igor/cognition/ (possibly NE integration for LLM call)
**Design rules:** theigors/rules/memory; theigors/rules/database; theigors/rules/budget (LLM call per engram — pacing required)
**Scope boundary:** In scope: habit definition + creation-time hook + retroactive sweep mode + budget pacing. Out of scope: changing engram schema (T-engram-purpose-schema), changing audit tooling.
**Test plan:** New engram created → purpose fields populated; retroactive sweep processes N unannotated engrams in one call; budget pacing respected (configurable batch size); unit test habit fires correctly.
